executive_summary: Today's top VC sourcing signals for 2026-04-11 indicate a strong presence of potential founders in India, with a notable lead being Dale Vaz, who is stealthily working on a Fintech venture, scoring 85 on our rubric. The majority of signals are concentrated in India, with a smaller but still significant presence in Southeast Asia, particularly in Singapore. Our strongest archetypes are second-time founders and senior executives with 10+ years of experience, with Lakshmi Nandan Reddy being another compelling lead, working on a Food Delivery/Logistics venture, scoring 75. Overall, these signals suggest a promising pipeline of potential investments, with Dale Vaz and Lakshmi Nandan Reddy emerging as two of the most compelling leads to watch.

persons.name: Dale Vaz
persons.investment_thesis: Dale Vaz, former CTO of Swiggy, is reportedly raising a significant round for his new stock trading app, Sahi. This indicates a strong founder with deep operational experience in a major tech company, now building in the high-growth Fintech sector in India. The substantial funding news is a strong positive signal.

signals.description: [Google News] Ex-Swiggy CTO’s stock trading app Sahi said to raise up to $80m - Tech in Asia

key_strengths: Previous CTO of a major tech company (Swiggy)
Building a new company (Sahi) in a high-growth sector (Fintech)
risks: Second-time founder status not explicitly confirmed (though implied by building a new company)

persons.name: Lakshmi Nandan Reddy
persons.investment_thesis: Lakshmi Nandan Reddy, a co-founder of Swiggy, has resigned. This signals a strong potential for a new venture, likely leveraging his deep experience in the food delivery and logistics space. While details are scarce, his track record makes him a compelling candidate for a second-time founder.

Her role suggests significant experience in identifying and backing startups, making her a potentially valuable founder.

risks: No information on current company or next venture
